title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: Market Wrap-up for June 18, 2026, and Weekend Outlook

items.headline: U.S. Markets Close Week Higher Amid Iran Peace Deal Optimism; Fed Hints at Potential Rate Hike
items.summary: U.S. equity markets concluded the abbreviated trading week on a positive note, with major indices posting gains on Thursday, June 18, 2026. The optimism stemmed largely from the interim peace agreement between the U.S. and Iran, which eased geopolitical tensions and drove down oil prices. However, a hawkish stance from the Federal Reserve, hinting at a potential rate hike later this year, introduced a degree of caution. Trading was closed on Friday, June 19, in observance of the Juneteenth holiday.

items.headline: Intel Surges on Apple Chip Production News
items.summary: Intel Corporation saw a significant jump after President Trump announced the semiconductor giant would produce chips for Apple in the U.S., boosting the broader chip sector.

items.headline: SanDisk Leads Gains
items.summary: SanDisk was among the top performers, contributing to the strong showing in the technology sector.

items.headline: Super Micro Computer Posts Double-Digit Rise
items.summary: Super Micro Computer continued its upward trend, benefiting from the strong demand in technology.

items.headline: IBM Declines Amid Broader Market Gains
items.summary: International Business Machines experienced a notable decline despite the overall positive market sentiment.

items.headline: Lockheed Martin Impacted by Peace Deal
items.summary: Defense contractor Lockheed Martin fell, as the U.S.-Iran peace agreement triggered a sector-wide selloff in defense stocks and the company missed revenue and earnings estimates.

items.headline: Johnson & Johnson Slips
items.summary: Johnson & Johnson was among the day's decliners.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Signals Hawkish Stance in First Warsh Meeting
items.summary: The Federal Reserve, under new Chair Kevin Warsh, maintained its benchmark interest rate at 3.5% to 3.75%. However, a hawkish shift in the 'dot plot' indicated that half of the officials anticipate at least one rate hike by the end of 2026, a significant change in guidance.

items.headline: U.S. and Iran Sign Interim Peace Agreement, Reopening Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: An interim peace deal between the United States and Iran was formally signed, leading to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. This development has been a key factor in the recent decline of oil prices and has boosted investor confidence in the broader U.S. stock market. The agreement, however, also led to a selloff in defense stocks due to altered defense spending expectations.

items.headline: AI Spending Continues to Fuel Market Gains
items.summary: Expectations for Artificial Intelligence (AI) data center spending have dramatically increased, providing a significant boost across various sectors including manufacturing, construction, materials, and energy. Companies benefiting from the AI spending boom, particularly memory chip makers and AI server providers, have been among the year's top performers.

items.headline: Inflation Outpaces Wages, Consumer Confidence Dips
items.summary: Despite strong employment data, inflation continues to remain elevated, with reports indicating that inflation is currently higher than wage growth. This disparity is contributing to a slide in consumer confidence and weakening real income, even as consumer spending remains steady.

items.headline: Market Valuations Approach Historic Highs, Raising Caution
items.summary: Stock market valuations, as measured by the Shiller P/E Ratio, are nearing all-time highs, approaching a record set in the early 1870s. This elevated valuation level is prompting warnings about the potential for a significant market correction of 20% or more.
